questions.question: Which action earns the most credits per single occurrence, all else being equal?

answers.text: Sending one ordinary message
answers.feedback: A plain message earns a flat +1 via message karma — the smallest of the three.

answers.text: Receiving one reaction on a message
answers.feedback: A reaction earns +3, more than a message, but well below the top end of a well-evidenced nark.

answers.text: Filing a nark with a named target, a concrete claim, and a real receipt
answers.feedback: Correct. A fully evidenced nark can earn up to +25 credits — far more than a single message or reaction.

answers.text: Filing a nark on yourself
answers.feedback: Self-narks have no real basis and are treated as vibes-only — they get fined, not rewarded.

questions.question: Which of these narks would most likely be ruled 'vibes-only' and fined? (Select all that apply)

answers.text: "Someone in here always does this, everyone knows it" with no named target
answers.feedback: Correct. No named target and no concrete claim — classic vibes-only.

answers.text: Narking a specific member by name for a real, quoted claim they made yesterday, with a screenshot
answers.feedback: This nark satisfies all three requirements — named target, concrete claim, real receipt — so it would be rewarded, not fined.

answers.text: Narking yourself for attention, with no offence actually on file
answers.feedback: Correct. Self-narks with no real basis are explicitly called out as fine-worthy.

answers.text: Reporting a colleague for being unusually good at their job
answers.feedback: Correct. Being good at your job isn't an offence — this kind of nark gets fined for lacking a real basis.

questions.question: A member appeals a fine but Karen rules the appeal has no real argument attached. What's the most likely outcome?

answers.text: The appeal is automatically upheld out of politeness
answers.feedback: Karen's rulings depend on the quality of the appeal — an empty argument doesn't get automatic sympathy.

answers.text: The appeal is denied, and may come with an extra 'court cost' fine
answers.feedback: Correct. Appeals are judged by the same receipt-based standard as narks — a weak or empty appeal typically gets denied and can backfire with an extra fine.

answers.text: The original fine is doubled automatically regardless of the appeal's content
answers.feedback: Extra penalties are tied to a backfiring appeal, not applied automatically to every appeal regardless of content.

answers.text: The case is forwarded to a community vote
answers.feedback: There's no voting step in the appeals process — Karen alone decides the outcome.

questions.question: What triggers a public 'Nark Credit obituary' message in the channel?

answers.text: Any credit loss at all, no matter how small
answers.feedback: Small losses don't trigger an obituary — there's a specific threshold involved.

answers.text: A single loss of 250 credits or more, from any source
answers.feedback: Correct. Fines, bad bets, and the guillotine can all trigger the obituary once a single loss hits 250 or more.

answers.text: Only losses caused specifically by a bad nark fine
answers.feedback: The obituary isn't limited to nark fines — any sufficiently large loss, including bad bets, can trigger it.

answers.text: Reaching a zero balance for the first time
answers.feedback: The trigger is the size of a single loss (250+), not reaching a specific balance milestone.

questions.question: Which best explains why message karma (+1) and reaction credits (+3) exist alongside the narking system?

answers.text: They were an early prototype that was later fully replaced by narking
answers.feedback: Both mechanics run side by side — passive earning wasn't replaced, it's still the largest source of ledger activity.

answers.text: They ensure ordinary, active participation is rewarded, not just successful call-outs
answers.feedback: Correct. Rewarding messages and reactions means the economy isn't purely a narking game — genuine participation matters too.

answers.text: They exist only to help Clawd calculate fines more accurately
answers.feedback: Message karma and reactions are independent earning streams, not inputs into how fines are calculated.

answers.text: They're only awarded to community owners, not regular members
answers.feedback: Message karma and reaction credits are earned by any active member, not restricted to owners.
